Tests if an empty string is equal to a new string object.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that checks if an empty `String` object is strictly equal to another empty `String` object created with the same constructor. The `assert` function is used to verify that the expression `String() === String("")` evaluates to `true`. This test ensures that the JavaScript `String` constructor behaves consistently when called without arguments and when called with an empty string argument. The code provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific behavior of the `String` constructor in JavaScript.
